title: aprs_synth.py: _get_key attempts lowercase fallback incorrectly
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/kiss/aprs_synth.py
Lines: 371-379

The _get_key helper function has a bug in its lowercase fallback:

```python
def _get_key(obj: dict, keys: tuple[str, ...]) -> Any:
    for k in keys:
        if k in obj:
            return obj[k]
        # Try lowercase
        if k.lower() in obj:
            return obj[k.lower()]  # BUG: should be obj[k.lower()] 
    return None
```

Wait, actually looking again - the code says `return obj[k.lower()]` which IS correct. Let me re-read...

Actually the issue is different. If `k` is already lowercase (e.g., 'temp'), then `k.lower()` is the same as `k`, so we check the same key twice. Not a bug per se, just redundant.

Let me look for other issues instead...
